title: writecell
summary: Write heterogeneous cell arrays to delimited text or spreadsheet files.

gpu_support.notes: Runs on the CPU. gpuArray values inside cells are gathered automatically before serialisation.

description: `writecell(C, filename)` serializes a 2-D cell array to delimited text or an Excel-compatible spreadsheet, preserving mixed numeric, logical, text, and empty cells.
behaviors: Accepts 2-D cell arrays containing numeric scalars, logical scalars, string scalars, row character vectors, and empty numeric or logical arrays.
Nested cell arrays, complex values, and non-scalar arrays produce descriptive errors so unsupported data is not silently flattened.

File type is inferred from the filename extension. `.xls`, `.xlsx`, `.xlsm`, `.xlsb`, and `.ods` select spreadsheet output; other extensions select delimited text unless `'FileType'` overrides the choice.
Delimited text follows MATLAB-compatible delimiter defaults: comma for `.csv`, tab for `.tsv` and `.tab`, whitespace for `.txt`, `.dat`, and `.dlm`, and comma otherwise.
`'Delimiter'`, `'QuoteStrings'`, `'WriteMode'`, and `'LineEnding'` control text output. `'WriteMode','append'` appends rows to text files.

Spreadsheet output writes a single worksheet and supports `'Sheet'` and `'Range'` for worksheet name/index and top-left placement. Spreadsheet append is rejected until in-place workbook mutation is supported.

examples.description: Append rows to a pipe-delimited text file
Write to a spreadsheet sheet and range

faqs.question: Does `writecell` support heterogeneous data?
faqs.answer: Yes. It is the tabular export path for mixed numeric, logical, text, and empty cell content.

faqs.question: Can `writecell` write Excel files?
faqs.answer: Yes. RunMat writes minimal OOXML workbooks for Excel-compatible extensions and supports worksheet names and top-left ranges.

faqs.question: How are strings quoted in CSV output?
faqs.answer: Strings are quoted by default and embedded double quotes are doubled. Set `'QuoteStrings', false` to write text values verbatim.

faqs.question: Can I append to an existing `.xlsx` file?
faqs.answer: Not yet. Text append is supported. Spreadsheet append currently returns a clear option error rather than mutating an existing workbook partially.

gpu_residency: `writecell` is a file-I/O sink. Any GPU-resident tensors encountered in cells are gathered before formatting so the file reflects host-visible values.
gpu_behavior: `writecell` does not launch provider kernels. It gathers resident inputs through the active acceleration provider and performs serialization on the host.
